commit.message:
test(workflows): expand workflow coverage + add CI coverage gate

- Add WorkflowCoverageExpansionTests (58 new tests) covering:
  - WorkflowSemVersion: TryParse (valid/invalid), pre-release, build
    metadata, comparison operators, CaretUpperBound, TildeUpperBound
  - WorkflowVersionSelector (via SelectVersion): latest, exact, ^caret,
    ~tilde, range constraints (>=, <), empty collection, all-deprecated
  - WorkflowVersioning.DetectBreakingChanges: no change, removed step,
    kind change, target change, condition change
  - WorkflowMatcher: exact name (score=1.0), tag overlap, no match,
    empty catalog, empty tags
  - TagWorkflowMatcher: best tag score wins, whitespace tags filtered
  - FileWorkflowCatalog: save/get round-trip, timestamp, list, get
    non-existent, delete by name, delete by version, breaking change
    enforcement, major bump allowed, empty dir
  - InMemoryWorkflowCatalog: save/get, specific version, list-latest,
    delete specific, delete all, breaking change enforcement, not-found

- Update pr-validation.yml (closes #242): add 'Coverage gate' step that:
  - Detects changed runtime source files (src/**/*.cs)
  - Fails if no test files were also changed
  - Allows bypass via 'coverage-override' PR label
  - Generates Cobertura report via ReportGenerator

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: fix Node.js workflow for monorepo structure

The default workflow expected a root-level package-lock.json and ran
npm ci/test at the repo root. This repo has no root package.json —
server/ and client/ each have their own.

Split into two jobs (server, client), each pointing cache and
working-directory at the correct subdirectory.

commit.message:
fix: use curl -T for streaming upload instead of --data-binary

--data-binary (even with @-/stdin) buffers the full payload in memory
before sending, causing OOM on large images.

-T (--upload-file) streams the file directly using the on-disk file
size for Content-Length, avoiding any in-memory buffering.
Combined with -X POST to keep the HTTP method correct.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: remove config bloat — dashboard/tunnel always on, use flags to disable

Remove three config fields that are now redundant:
- dashboard.enabled: always true, use --no-dashboard to disable
- dashboard.auto_start_tunnel: always true, use --no-tunnel to disable
- dashboard.tunnel_allow_emails: was dead code (logged but never
  applied ACLs)

These are now runtime-only fields (json:"-") set by DefaultConfig
and overridden by CLI flags. The config file is simpler — only real
user settings remain.

Also:
- Flip env var propagation from opt-in (OTTO_DASHBOARD=1) to
  opt-out (OTTO_NO_DASHBOARD=1) matching the new defaults
- Preserve runtime-only fields across config merge round-trips
- Update README config reference table

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(tests): stabilize flaky tests and expand coverage (#247)

- Fix ProviderDetectorTests: make FindCli_FindsDotnet and FindCli_FindsGit
  skip gracefully when tools are not on PATH (environmental, not code bugs)
- Fix AgentLoopTracingTests: replace List<Activity> with ConcurrentBag<Activity>
  to prevent collection-modified exceptions during parallel test execution;
  use Assert.Contains instead of Assert.Single for thread safety
- Add CliHandlerTests: 16 new tests covering AgentsCliHandler,
  PluginCliHandler, and PolicyComplianceCliHandler routing, help text,
  argument validation, and error handling (all at 0% prior coverage)
- Add ToolConfirmationFilterTests: 10 new tests covering BuildRedactedArgs
  (null, empty, sensitive key redaction, value truncation, multi-key)
  and ResolvePolicyToolName (unknown tools, null safety)

Closes #246 (flaky tests)
Addresses #240 (CLI handler coverage)
Addresses #241 (ToolConfirmationFilter coverage)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: clearly inform user when tunnel is skipped due to missing tools

When dashboard.auto_start_tunnel is true (the default) but bgtask
or devtunnel aren't installed, otto now logs a clear warning with
install instructions instead of silently skipping or failing with
a cryptic error.

Messages appear in three places:
- tunnel.Manager.Start(): warns and returns nil (graceful skip)
- dashboard server auto-start: warns before skipping
- daemon printStatus(): shows 'tunnel: skipped (reason)' to the
  user immediately after 'otto server start'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: stream image upload via stdin to avoid curl OOM on large files

--data-binary @file loads the entire file into memory before sending,
which fails with 'out of memory' for large images (1.2G+).

Fix: use --data-binary @- and redirect the file via stdin, which makes
curl stream the data without buffering the whole file in memory.

Applied to both harvester-vm.sh (_do_upload) and image/main.tf (terraform_data).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: dashboard and tunnel enabled by default, replace --dashboard-only with --no-pr-monitoring

Simplify the startup model: dashboard and tunnel are on by default
with an empty config. Users opt out with --no-dashboard, --no-tunnel,
or --no-pr-monitoring instead of opting in.

Changes:
- DefaultConfig: dashboard.enabled=true, auto_start_tunnel=true
- Remove --dashboard-only flag, add --no-pr-monitoring
- Simplify CLI auto-enable logic (just read config booleans)
- Add NoPRMonitoring field to ServerConfig (runtime-only, json:"-")
- Gate RunMonitorLoop on NoPRMonitoring flag
- Update README command reference and config defaults

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add critical unit tests for Tier 1 and Tier 2 targets

  success path, OrasUsageException vs OrasException catch ordering

  failure on missing helper binary, cancellation token handling

fix(wasm): append QWT_CONFIG -= directives instead of sed-deleting lines

The sed-delete approach (sed -i '/QwtConcurrent/d') was unreliable:
qmake sub-directory builds re-read qwtconfig.pri fresh, but multi-
feature lines or includes from other .pri files could still add the
feature back. Appending 'QWT_CONFIG -= <feat>' at the END of
qwtconfig.pri is guaranteed to win because qmake processes the file
sequentially — a trailing -= always overrides any earlier +=.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: strip surrounding quotes from kubeconfig server/token values

The _kc_field() regex \S+ matched the surrounding YAML quotes when the
kubeconfig value was written as:
  server: "https://harvester-hpc.edge.suse.org/k8s/clusters/local"

This caused the extracted server value to be
  "https://..." (with literal quote characters)
producing a malformed curl URL and HTTP code 000 on every poll,
so _do_upload() always timed out after 5 minutes.

Fix: strip leading/trailing quote characters (", ') from the
extracted value in both the Python3 path (.strip('"\''))) and the
awk fallback (gsub(/["\047]/,"",...)).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Device Linking DL-1 — InitiateDeviceLink, ConfirmDeviceLink, ListDevices, RevokeDevice

- proto: add DeviceLinkService with ConfirmDeviceLink RPC
- proto: add InitiateDeviceLink RPC to DeviceService
- proto: add InitiateDeviceLinkRequest/Response, ConfirmDeviceLinkRequest messages
- construct-queue: add store/consume_device_link_token (15 min Redis TTL, one-time use)
- construct-db: add deactivate_device() soft-delete function
- auth-service: implement InitiateDeviceLink (generates 32-byte random base64url token)
- auth-service: implement ConfirmDeviceLink (validates token, creates device, issues JWT)
- auth-service: implement ListDevices (streams active devices for authenticated user)
- auth-service: implement RevokeDevice (deactivates device, revokes Redis sessions)
- auth-service: register DeviceLinkServiceServer in gRPC router

fix: automation content quality — comment pipeline, reconciliation, topics, bans, daily cap

- Add try/catch around fetch_discussions_for_commenting() with fallback to
  discussions_cache.json so comments/votes still happen when GraphQL fails
- Auto-fix state drift: write_autonomy_log now calls reconcile_counts()
  instead of just logging verify_consistency() mismatches
- Replace 15 navel-gazing suggested_topics with 25 diverse real-world topics
- Inject ALL banned phrases into LLM prompt (was only first 15 of 60+)
- Add DAILY_POST_CAP (50/day) — redirects post→comment when cap reached
  to prevent wild volume swings (4 to 165 posts/day observed)

commit.message:
foundations: fill gaps in linear algebra, vector calculus, SLT, and deep learning theory

01_linear_algebra/03_eigenvalues:
- Add svd.md: full/thin SVD, Eckart-Young theorem, pseudoinverse, PCA connection
- Add spectral_theorem.md: symmetric diagonalization, PSD, Cholesky, Rayleigh quotient
- Update index.md with 1-line descriptions for all 5 notes

02_calculus_and_analysis/05_vector_calculus:
- Add gradient_and_directional_derivative.md: ∇f, level sets, ML optimization connection
- Add jacobian_and_hessian.md: vector chain rule, second-order conditions, Newton's method
- Update index.md

05_statistical_learning_theory:
- Add pac_learning.md: PAC/agnostic PAC framework, sample complexity, ERM
- Add vc_dimension.md: shattering, growth function, Sauer-Shelah, fundamental theorem
- Add generalization_bounds.md: Rademacher complexity, uniform convergence, SRM, PAC-Bayes
- Update index.md with formal-theory section and 1-line descriptions

06_deep_learning_theory:
- Add activation_functions.md: sigmoid/tanh/ReLU/GELU/Swish, universal approximation
- Add dropout.md: inverted dropout, MC dropout, ensemble interpretation
- Add layer_normalization.md: LN vs BN, Pre-LN, RMSNorm, transformer placement
- Add residual_connections.md: gradient highway, ensemble theory, DenseNet, Highway nets
- Move style_cost_function.md → 02_modeling/03_model_families/04_neural_networks/
- Update index.md (remove style_cost_function, add 4 new notes, reorganise sections)
- Promote status seed→growing: backpropagation, weight_initialization, adaptive_optimizers, batch_normalization

02_modeling/03_model_families/04_neural_networks:
- Add style_cost_function.md (moved from foundations, layer frontmatter corrected)
- Update index.md to include style_cost_function entry

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix ruff format and mypy type annotation errors in test_image_gen_tool.py (#325)

Two CI lint failures in `tests/unit_tests/test_image_gen_tool.py`
introduced with the image gen tool.

## Changes

- **ruff format**: Rewrote multi-context `with` statement to
parenthesized form:
  ```python
  # before
  with patch("langchain_azure_ai.tools.image_gen.os") as mock_os, patch(
      "openai.OpenAI"
  ) as mock_cls:

  # after
  with (
      patch("langchain_azure_ai.tools.image_gen.os") as mock_os,
      patch("openai.OpenAI") as mock_cls,
  ):
  ```

- **mypy `no-untyped-def`**: Added missing return type annotations and
imported `Generator`:
  - `mock_openai_client` → `Generator[tuple[Any, Any, Any], None, None]`
  - `_make_tool` → `tuple[Any, Any]`
